Question 1: Part-A
A- Apache Hadoop is a tool which is used for data analysis.
* It helps us to retrieve & get the data.
* It helps us to keep data in organised format.

Question 2: Data Serialization
A- Data Serialization is a process where we keep the data in organised manner.
* It gives a node to each block.
* We can identify the data with the key value.
* Later... the data is arranged in ascending or descending order.
* Data is arranged as per Serial Number.

Question 3: Hadoop
A- Hadoop is Java based software which is used for data analysis.
* It helps in various data analytics techniques.
* It is used for data cleaning also.
* It helps us to retrieve & get the data.
* It helps us to keep data in organised format.

Question 4: Data Node
A- Data Node is most important component in HDFS daemons.
* It comes in third position after the Name Node & Secondary Node.
* It helps us to assign Nodes for the data.
* We can retrieve data in that manner.

Question 5: Machine learning
A- ML is a technique where we provide external training to particular model.
* Based on the training the model provide accurate decisions.
* It also provides results as per the accuracy.
* It helps in doing repetitive tasks & automate the human tasks.
* Types of machine learning techniques:
1) Supervised learning
2) Unsupervised learning
3) Semi supervised learning
4) Reinforcement learning

Question 6: Part-B
HDFS daemons
A- HDFS - Hadoop distributed File System.
* It is a tool which is used for data analysis.
* It helps us to retrieve & get the data.
* It helps us to keep data in organised format.
* It works with different Software where we perform different operations.
* It also helps in various data analytics techniques.
* It also helps in data Cleaning, data processing, pre-processing.
* There are various components which helps in it.

Question 6 (continued): Various components.
1) Name Node
2) Secondary Node
3) Data Node
4) Resource Manager
5) Node manager.

[Diagram: Block diagram showing Name Node connected to Secondary Node and Data Node. Secondary Node and Data Node are connected to Resource Manager and Node Manager.]

* Name Mode
* Name Node is used to assign the Name to the node.
* It is one of the main components in HDFS.
* It is the first component in the HDFS.
* From here only data takes entry.
* It responsible for input process.

Question 6 (continued): * Secondary Mode
* It is mainly used to provide backup for Name Node.
* It is also used to merge with Name Node & Support in absence.
* Input format: $<$Key: Value$>$ , $<$Key_2: Value_2$>$
* Output format: $<$Key: Value$>$

* Data Node
* It come in third place after the Name node & Secondary Node.
* It helps us to assign nodes for the data.
* We can easily retrieve data in that manner.
* Data is retrieved easily with the key. value.
* Data is arranged as per the serial.

Question 6 (continued): number in ascending or descending order..

* Resource Manager
* It provides all the important Resources required for hard & Software required.
* It make necessary arrangements if anything is missing.
* It manages with all the resources.

* Node Manager
* It manages all the nodes.
* It helps in coordination with other components & the nodes.
A. It is used to maintain data with different nodes.
* It helps with memory & data management.

Question 8: Machine learning techniques
A- ML is a technique where we provide external training to particular model.
* Based on the training the model provide accurate decisions.
* It also provides results as per the accuracy.
* It helps in doing repetitive tasks & automate the human tasks.
* Types of ML techniques:
1) Supervised learning
2) Unsupervised learning
3) Semi Supervised learning
4) Reinforcement learning

Question 8 (continued): * Supervised learning.
* Here the model is trained with Various data sets.
* It takes the data as per the dataset.
* It is trained as per classified data.
* It is used when there are enough data sets.
Ex- House price prediction.

* Unsupervised learning
* Here the model is not trained with dataset.
* Here we give new data as raw data for training purpose.
* We might get different results for it.
Ex- Detection of Cyber bullying.

Question 8 (continued): * Semi Supervised learning
* This technique is combination of both.
* Here the model is trained with new data & old datasets as well.
* It takes both old data & new data.
* It can be trained as per the dataset which results in various results.
* We might also get different results.
Ex- loan approval prediction System.

* Reinforcement learning.
* It is a special condition for ML.
* Here we automate the tasks as per the requirements.
* It is used for IOT Purpose also.
* Ex- Human Scream detection using deep learning.

Question 9: Social Media Analytics
A- Social Media Analytics plays a very important role in day to day life.
* Many people use social media every day.
* Apps like instagram, facebook etc.
* It tracks our usage.
* It controls our activities.
* It also collects our data for further usage.
* It is used in every Social media Platform.
* It reduces cyberbullying in social media.
* Also reduces cyber crimes.
* We can report fake account easily.

Question 9 (continued): Importance
* To track our usage.
* To Limit overuse.
* To Reduce cyber bullying.
* It reduces Cyber crimes.
* It helps to report fake accounts.

Applications
* Targeted ads - Show only required ads.
* Customer acquisition - To acquire more ads.
* Feedback - To provide feedbacks.
* Product Recommendation - To recommend the product as per the requirements.
* Market analysis - To analyze different products.

Question 10: | Traditional Analytics | Big data Analytics |
|

Question 10 (continued): Ex- Post office | Ex- Data Science company

* Traditional Analytics
* It does not uses R programming.
* It uses manual process.
* More paper work.
* Labour work.

* Big data Analytics
* It uses R programming.
* Along with R it uses various languages like python.
* Less paper work.
* No labour work.

Question 10 (continued): R programming
* It is a Scripting language similar to python.
* It is used for data analysis.

[Graph: A line starting from the top-left and decreasing linearly towards the bottom-right corner.]
